prompt: Using REFERENCE_0 as the visual foundation, expand the single sky-palace scene into a complete cinematic world-building presentation for a fantasy civilization. Preserve the same East Asian celestial imperial design language, floating architecture above clouds, gilded ornamentation, warm sunset lighting, and ultra-detailed realistic concept-art look, then turn it into a polished multi-panel lore board on a dark background with elegant editorial typography. Include 8 labeled sections: 1 large hero overview panel for the civilization, 1 architecture section with 9 images, 1 characters section with 7 full-body character designs, 1 clothing and armor section with 6 outfit variations, 1 vehicles and transport section with 4 vehicle designs, 1 world map section with 1 illustrated map, 1 environments section with 6 environment images, and 1 culture and details section with 6 close-up detail images. Add a refined fantasy title and short lore paragraph for the civilization, using the name Celestial Dynasty. The architecture section should show 9 distinct items: palace, temple, gate, pillar, bridge, throne hall, garden, library, and observatory. The characters section should show 7 archetypes: emperor, empress, high priest, general, royal guard, scholar, and artisan. The clothing and armor section should show 6 designs: ceremonial robe, battle armor, noble attire, priest robes, artisan garments, and dancer costume. The vehicles and transport section should show 4 designs: an airship, a flying beast mount, a sky chariot, and a ceremonial palanquin. The environments section should show 6 scenes: one wide banner panorama plus 5 smaller locations, including forest, mountain peaks, lotus valley, golden fields, and a celestial port. The culture and details section should show 6 images: dragon motif, lantern or artifact, palace interior, scholarly scene, mechanical instruments, and decorative symbols. Keep all panels visually unified as if from one official franchise world bible, with extremely rich detail, believable material design, and cinematic realism.
